description:
Implement the skeleton of sync that polls GH issues and decides which
SyncReport entry (if any) each one warrants. This child does not yet
emit created/updated/deleted — it only resolves identity and decides
"is this a candidate for an entry, or skip?". B4b/c/d each consume that
decision matrix to emit one entry kind.

Identity resolution (in order):
1. Match by stored external.github_issues.number on any balls task →
   known mapping.
2. Match by [bl-xxxx] substring in GH issue title → known mapping
   (recovery path when number is somehow lost).
3. Otherwise → unmatched (candidate for B4c auto-create).

Loop avoidance: any matched issue whose latest balls-side
external.github_issues.synced_at >= GH updated_at is SILENTLY SKIPPED —
balls already saw this state. This is what stops the push→pull echo.

WHY: doing identity + loop avoidance once, here, is what makes B4b/c/d
each a clean "this matched issue triggered this entry kind" leaf. If
this logic lived inline in B4b/c/d, three copies would drift and the
loop-avoidance bug would surface as a duplicate-create or
ping-pong-status.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A `classify(issue, tasks) -> Classification` function exported by
  pull.rs returning one of: Skip(reason), KnownUpdate(task_id),
  AutoCreate, KnownDelete(task_id) (B4d will produce KnownDelete).
- Mockito tests cover: number-match, tag-match, no-match,
  synced_at-skip, malformed title, missing fields.
- The pull skeleton runs sync end-to-end emitting an empty SyncReport;
  classification is exercised by tests but no entries are emitted yet.
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Pull skeleton: identity matching + loop avoidance
